Front: What was the Cold War?
Back: A period of political ideological and military tension between the USA and the USSR

Front: When did the Cold War begin?
What ideological differences divided the USA and USSR?
Who led the USA during the early Cold War?
Who led the Soviet Union after World War II?
What was the Yalta Conference?
Back: A meeting between Allied leaders in 1945 to discuss post war Europe

Front: Which leaders attended Yalta?
What agreement was made about Germany at Yalta?
Back: Germany would be divided into occupation zones

Front: What was the Potsdam Conference?
Back: A meeting of Allied leaders after Germany's defeat

Front: Why did tensions rise at Potsdam?
Back: Disagreements over the future of Europe

Front: What was the Iron Curtain?
Back: A term describing the division between Eastern and Western Europe

Front: Who used the term Iron Curtain?
What was the Truman Doctrine?
Back: A US policy to contain the spread of communism

Front: When was the Truman Doctrine announced?
What was the Marshall Plan?
Back: US economic aid to rebuild Western Europe

Front: Why did the USSR oppose the Marshall Plan?
Back: It increased American influence in Europe

Front: What organisation did the USSR create in response?
What was COMECON?
Back: An economic organisation of communist countries

Front: What was NATO?
Back: A military alliance of Western countries

Front: When was NATO formed?
What was the Warsaw Pact?
Back: A military alliance of communist countries

Front: When was the Warsaw Pact formed?
What caused the Berlin Blockade?
Back: The USSR blocked land routes to West Berlin

Front: When did the Berlin Blockade occur?
How did the West respond to the blockade?
What was the Berlin Airlift?
Why was the Berlin Airlift significant?
Back: It demonstrated Western commitment to Berlin

Front: What war marked the first major Cold War conflict?
When did the Korean War begin?
Why did the Korean War begin?
Which countries supported South Korea?
Back: The United States and United Nations forces

Front: Which country supported North Korea?
What was the outcome of the Korean War?
Back: Korea remained divided at the 38th parallel

Front: Who became leader of the USSR after Stalin?
What policy did Khrushchev introduce?
What crisis occurred in Hungary in 1956?
Why did the Soviet Union invade Hungary?
Back: To maintain control over Eastern Europe

Front: What was the Berlin Wall?
Back: A barrier dividing East and West Berlin

Front: When was the Berlin Wall built?
Why was the Berlin Wall built?
Back: To stop East Germans fleeing to the West

Front: What was the Cuban Missile Crisis?
Back: A confrontation between the USA and USSR over nuclear missiles in Cuba

Front: When did the Cuban Missile Crisis occur?
Who led the USA during the crisis?
Who led the USSR during the crisis?
Why was the Cuban Missile Crisis dangerous?
Back: It brought the world close to nuclear war

Front: How was the Cuban Missile Crisis resolved?
Back: The USSR removed missiles from Cuba

Front: What was détente?
Back: A period of reduced tensions between the USA and USSR

Front: When did détente occur?
What agreement limited nuclear weapons in the 1970s?
What did SALT stand for?
What was the Helsinki Accords?
Back: An agreement improving relations between East and West

Front: What event increased Cold War tensions in 1979?
Why did the USSR invade Afghanistan?
What was the US response to the invasion?
Who became US president in 1981?
Why did Reagan increase military spending?
Back: To pressure the Soviet Union economically

Front: Who became Soviet leader in 1985?
What policy introduced openness in the USSR?
What policy restructured the Soviet economy?
Why did Gorbachev introduce reforms?
What happened to communist governments in Eastern Europe in 1989?
What event symbolised the end of division in Germany?
Back: The fall of the Berlin Wall

Front: When did the Berlin Wall fall?
When did the Soviet Union collapse?
Why did the Soviet Union collapse?
Back: Economic problems political reforms and nationalist movements

Front: Why was the Cold War historically significant?
Back: It shaped global politics for nearly fifty years
